action.reasoning: Observation: Best result is safe_follow_steps=54.3 (iter 9) with ang=-K*angle. The main bottleneck is maintaining the safe zone (1-2m, facing human) for long enough. I need to: 1) Check the actual facing threshold from config (might be different from my assumed 45deg), 2) Add depth-based obstacle avoidance for navigation, 3) Improve the following controller with better gains and PD control, 4) Use humanoid_detector to distinguish human from obstacles. Analysis: The robot finds the human 72% of the time (nav_to_pos_success) but only maintains safe zone for 54 steps. The facing control and distance maintenance need improvement. Also, obstacles may block the path. Decision: Write improved predict.py with depth obstacle avoidance, better PD control, and config-based facing threshold. Also investigate the actual facing threshold value.
